title: BC Hydro turns Electric School Buses into Mobile Power Sources in Canadian First
content: BC Hydro has become the first Canadian utility to pilot vehicle-to-grid technology in real-world conditions, partnering with Lynch Bus Lines and Fuse Power Management to test electric school buses that can both draw power and feed electricity back into the grid. Each 200 kWh bus battery can power 75 homes for nearly two hours, providing backup power during outages and easing peak demand. The project, backed by $200,000 from BC Hydro, $860,000 from NorthX Climate Tech, $50,000 from PacifiCan, and buses on loan from RIDE Canada, follows a successful 2023 lab demo and marks a major step in B.C.â€™s clean energy innovation. BC Hydro plans to expand the program with more charging hubs to maximize EVs as mobile grid resources.
author: Analysts Account
